methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Senra Systems is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 comparable companies at Series A and Series B stages in robotics and manufacturing, supplemented by a broader universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $80M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M (Fairmat, Series B) to $10.0B (1X Technologies, Series B). The peer set captures meaningful heterogeneity in stage and scale within the sector.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $68M to $1.2B, with a median estimate of $578M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in comparable selection and stage adjustment. The stage-progression floor assumes minimal additional value creation between Series A close and next institutional round. The peer-median ceiling captures full-stage comparables but includes Series B companies with greater operational maturity and market validation. A defensible valuation range for Senra would center toward $300M–$800M, conditional on near-term revenue traction and the company's competitive position within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, customer concentration, and the timing and terms of Series B.
